executive_summary: Pre-monsoon hydrological assessment of the Yamuna Reservoir Zone reveals elevated flood risk due to compromised drainage infrastructure. Reservoir Alpha is at 78.2% capacity (seasonal low, expected). However, 2 of 5 drainage lines are partially blocked, reducing total drainage capacity by 35%. Storm Drain SD-7 is at critical capacity (48%). Urban construction has increased impermeable surface area by 12%, accelerating runoff velocity. Monsoon onset is estimated at 45 days. Immediate clearing of Primary Drainage D-1 and Storm Drain SD-7 is required to prevent track-level flooding at underpass Section 4.
ai_findings: Reservoir Alpha at 78.2% capacity — within normal seasonal range for pre-monsoon period
Yamuna Tributary Canal shows 12% sediment accumulation along southern bank — dredging recommended
Primary Drainage D-1 capacity reduced to 65% — sediment and construction debris blockage detected at 3 points
Storm Drain SD-7 at critical 48% capacity — complete blockage risk if not cleared before monsoon
Seasonal Pond N-3 formation detected (1,200 sq.m) — groundwater seepage may affect track stability in Section 7
Impermeable surface area increased 12% since 2023 baseline — runoff coefficient now exceeds design parameters

flood_risk.description: 35% drainage capacity reduction with monsoon 45 days away

embankment_integrity.description: Southern canal bank shows 8% erosion since January

recommendations.action: Clear Primary Drainage D-1 at 3 identified blockage points. Deploy mechanical excavation for sediment and construction debris removal.
Emergency clearing of Storm Drain SD-7 (48% capacity). Install temporary pumping station at underpass Section 4.
Dredge Yamuna Tributary Canal southern bank (12% sedimentation). Restore flow capacity before monsoon onset.
Repair 2 non-functional culverts at grid refs 28.628°N/77.220°E and 28.631°N/77.225°E.
Install water-level sensors at Reservoir Alpha, Drainage D-1 exit, and underpass Section 4 for real-time monsoon monitoring.
Commission runoff impact study for new impermeable surfaces. Evaluate mandatory rainwater harvesting for construction within 200m of drainage corridor.

environmental_insights.water_quality: Yamuna Tributary Canal — moderate turbidity, dissolved oxygen 4.2 mg/L (below 5.0 threshold)
environmental_insights.groundwater: Seasonal pond N-3 indicates rising water table in Section 7 — track stability monitoring required
environmental_insights.erosion: Southern canal bank erosion rate: 0.8m/year — embankment reinforcement needed within 18 months
environmental_insights.monsoon_readiness: Overall readiness: 42% — 3 critical drainage issues must be resolved before June 25 estimated onset

infrastructure_warnings: Track underpass Section 4 flooded 3 times during 2025 monsoon — same drainage issues persist
Culvert C-3 at km 4.2 showing structural cracking — load rating reduced to 60% pending repair
Canal embankment settlement detected near Reservoir Alpha outflow — 15cm subsidence over 6 months
